🌆 Market News Digest
[Jun 10, 2026 06:00-16:30 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• Trump escalates on Iran — warns of new strikes; Axios says he held Situation Room talks on larger Iran options, driving oil up and stocks down
• US CPI matches forecasts, core cools — headline CPI +4.2% y/y, core +2.9%; markets trimmed Fed hike bets as inflation remains energy-led
• Israel/Iran conflict widens — strikes, sirens, tanker interdictions and IAEA censure keep Hormuz risk premium elevated

⛽ Oil & Energy
• WTI $90.03, Brent $93.10 — crude rallied on Iran fears; EIA showed a bigger-than-expected crude draw and SPR stocks at a 2023 low
• Hormuz shipping risk rises — tanker “dark transits” increase, while Iran signals a Hormuz plan and US/CENTCOM tout blockade enforcement
• Energy shock driving inflation — gasoline/diesel spikes are doing most of the work behind the May CPI print

📊 Markets & Macro
• Risk-off hit US equities — S&P/Nasdaq slid sharply, tech entered correction, and gold erased over $1T in market cap amid liquidation
• Treasury yields edge higher — 10Y note auction solid, but yields stayed near 4.54% after CPI and oil shock
• BoC holds at 2.25% — Macklem flagged weak growth, sticky energy risk, and policy dependence on incoming data

🌍 Geopolitical
• Doha mediation continues — US and Iran held parallel talks via Qatari mediators, but Tehran rejected a trilateral meeting
• Middle East spreads — Gulf states condemned Iran, Israel warned of further force, and Erdogan cautioned against regional escalation
• EU/NATO tensions stay high — Germany pushes defense buildup; Europe’s view of the US as an ally has fallen sharply

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Stablecoin/agentic payments gain traction — OpenAI/Visa partnership and Mastercard’s agent payments push highlight AI-commerce rails
• Raydium exploit hit a retired AMM — $1.34M loss covered by treasury, adding to DeFi security concerns
• Crypto sentiment stays fearful — fear gauge at 9/100 as macro and geopolitics pressure risk assets

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• CFTC targets prediction markets — sweeping proposal could reshape fast-growing event contracts
• IAEA demands fuller Iran disclosure — board passed a resolution requiring access to enriched uranium stockpiles
• Trump signs Secure America Act / USMCA skepticism — more policy churn for trade, immigration and security

🏢 Corporate
• Oracle beats, but capex explodes — Q4 EPS/revenue topped estimates; 2027 capex seen at $90B and financing needs jump
• SpaceX IPO demand overwhelms supply — reportedly >4x oversubscribed, with Gulf funds bidding billions despite Warren/state pension pushback
• OpenAI keeps expanding — new model prep, IPO talk, and a soon-to-launch tender at $687.69/share
• Pfizer, Porsche, Novelis and Boeing-linked supply/news flow — Germany investment caution, 911 stays gas/hybrid, auto aluminum plant restart, and OEM supply easing

🌅 Market News Digest
[Jun 9–10, 2026 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• US escalates strikes on Iran; Iran hits back — fresh CENTCOM strikes in Iran met by missile/drone attacks on US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan; oil, rates and risk assets all jolted  
• Hormuz tensions lift oil, rattle markets — crude rebounded on supply-risk fears after attacks near the Strait of Hormuz; Shell flagged the market as “in the hole” on barrel availability  
• China CPI/PPI mixed, but inflation stays mild — May CPI 1.2% y/y and PPI 3.9% y/y kept deflation worries alive despite firmer factory-gate prices  
• BOJ Governor Ueda hospitalized — he’ll miss the June 15–16 meeting and submit a written statement, adding uncertainty around Japan policy timing  

⛽ Oil & Energy
• API crude stocks fell 9.1M bbl vs -3.4M est; Cushing also drew down, supporting crude  
• Iraq cut July official selling prices; Kazakhstan sees 2026 oil output at 98M tons  
• Deutsche Energy Terminal says Stade LNG floating terminal now starts in September  
• Shell says China is drawing on oil reserves and Hormuz disruption leaves the market “1.2B barrels in the hole”  

📊 Markets & Macro
• Asia-Pacific equities sold off on Iran shock + Wall St tech weakness: Nikkei, KOSPI, Taiwan and Singapore all lower  
• Korea’s KOSPI slid as much as 6.5%; Samsung fell 6.8% as risk aversion surged  
• Japan PPI accelerated to 6.3% y/y, the fastest since 2023; JGB yields rose then auction demand softened  
• Germany’s DIW cut 2026 growth to 0.5% and warned of a technical recession from the energy shock  

🌍 Geopolitical
• Iran confirmed calm after strikes, but later warned “decisive” retaliation and said diplomacy is being harmed  
• Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan activated air defenses; interceptions reportedly limited damage and casualties  
• China urged restraint in the Middle East and expressed “deep concern” over the war  
• Russia said it shot down 326 Ukrainian drones overnight; Ukraine hit facilities at Mariupol port  

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Botanix, a Bitcoin layer-2, said it will wind down and told users to withdraw assets  
• Japan’s biggest banks plan a joint stablecoin launch by March 2027  
• OpenAI-related infra/AI trade stayed hot: project news fed more appetite for data-center/compute themes  

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• White House AI policy adviser Thomas Lind is departing amid tech-policy staff churn  
• CFTC is preparing expansive new prediction-markets rules  
• South Korea launched FX-market inspections; Taiwan’s central bank flagged loan growth, inflows and possible CPI risks  
• UK is drafting tweaks to steel tariffs; Ireland was told to rein in corporate-tax windfall spending  

🏢 Corporate
• OpenAI is negotiating a massive 10GW Ohio data-center lease with Nvidia backing; separate reports say SoftBank’s $6B OpenAI margin loan is stalling  
• Meta agreed to lease 168MW from Reliance’s India data-center project and backed nearly 1GW of renewable power  
• TSMC May sales rose 30.1% y/y, reinforcing AI-chip strength  
• Honda is recalling 880,514 US vehicles; Renault wants a 10-year freeze on new EU car rules
